summary: A single Jest test assertion failed in `test/config/get.ts`. The test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` expected the registry URL `https://my-org.registry.example.com` (without a trailing slash) but received `https://my-org.registry.example.com/` (with a trailing slash), causing the entire CI job to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The `pnpm config get` command returns a registry URL with a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`) but the test expects the URL without a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com`). This `expect(received).toBe(expected)` mismatch in `test/config/get.ts:32` caused the test suite to fail.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: Explicitly marks this test file as failed.

evidence.quote: ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Identifies the specific failing test case.
Shows the exact assertion mismatch: expected no trailing slash, received a trailing slash.
Pinpoints the exact file and line where the assertion failed.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly 1 test suite and 1 test failed overall.
Shows the jest run exited with status 1 due to the test failure, propagating the failure to the CI job.

suggested_fix: Fix the `pnpm config get` command to strip trailing slashes from registry URLs before outputting them, or update the test expectation at `test/config/get.ts:32` to accept a trailing slash — whichever matches the intended behavior.
